title: BACARDÍ Superior Rum | White Rum | BACARDÍ Global
clean_text: BACARDÍ Superior Rum | White Rum | BACARDÍ Global OUR RUMS OUR RUMS White Rum BACARDÍ CARTA BLANCA Flavors Flavors Range Spiced BACARDÍ Spiced Dark Rum BACARDÍ Carta Oro BACARDÍ Carta Negra BACARDÍ Añejo Cuatro BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Diez BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Limitada ready to enjoy BACARDÍ x coca-cola Ready to Drink cocktails Ready to Serve cocktails BACARDÍ Coquito View all our rums Cocktails Experiences Experiences Casa BACARDÍ Puerto Rico Visit the World’s largest premium rum distillery. SUSTAINABILITY Our World FAQS COCKTAILS MOJITO White Rum BACARDÍ Carta Blanca In 1862, after 10 years of perfecting his rums, Don Facundo Bacardí Massó introduced the world to his BACARDÍ Carta Blanca rum. A sublime rum for cocktails, it doesn’t dominate other flavours nor does it disappear when mixed. Perfect for classic rum cocktails like the Mojito. VIDEO HOW TO MAKE A MOJITO A classic profile Tasting Notes Floral and fruity, BACARDÍ Carta Blanca rum serves up orange blossom, lavender and rose, teamed with apricot, lime, light coconut and ripe banana. Nose Almonds and fruit Palate Smooth and creamy Finish Dry, clean, fresh BACARDÍ CARTA BLANCA INSPIRED A COCKTAIL REVOLUTION AS THE FIRST MIXABLE RUM. PROCESS Filtered to perfection This distinctive spirit is aged in American white oak barrels and shaped through a secret blend of charcoal for a distinctive smoothness, making it perfect for classic rum cocktails. How to Serve THE PERFECT MIXER BACARDÍ Carta Blanca is a light and aromatic white rum with delicate floral and fruity notes, ideal for mixing. History The Rum Originals THE DAIQUIRÍ In 1898, American mining engineer Jennings Stockton Cox created the Daiquirí. Dining with a colleague, he mixed three local ingredients – lime juice, sugar and BACARDÍ Carta Blanca – with ice and chilled water. MAKE THE COCKTAIL BACARDÍ & Cola Like many of the world’s greatest pairings, a rum and cola is best if made with ‘the original’ BACARDÍ. MAKE THE COCKTAIL The Mojito The origin of the name ‘Mojito’ is shrouded in mystery. Some believe it comes from ‘mojar’, the Spanish verb ‘to wet’, whilst others say it comes from the West African word ‘mojo’ meaning to place a spell. MAKE THE COCKTAIL The Piña Colada The national cocktail of Puerto Rico, its creation is credited to Ramón ‘Monchito’ Marrero, a bartender at the Caribe Hilton Hotel’s Beachcomber’s Bar in San Juan. He first mixed it on August 15th, 1954. MAKE THE COCKTAIL DRAG TO DISCOVER MORE BACARDÍ Cocktails Make it Superior A rum destined for mixing. Unleash a world of new flavours and experiment with BACARDÍ Carta Blanca white rum in all your favourite cocktails. View all cocktails Mojito Daiquiri (shaken) BACARDÍ & Cola Piña Colada (shaken) Rum Punch Rum Mule Hurricane Daiquiri (frozen) STRAWBERRY DAIQUIRI COCKTAIL (FROZEN) BACARDÍ Coquito Zombie Mojito jug Blood Moon Drag to discover more View all cocktails SOCIAL MOVE WITH US A soft sway, or a lively dance. We have something for everyone. Follow us on social to stay up-to-date with the latest BACARDÍ news. #DoWhatMovesYou EXPLORE THE A-Z OF RUM COCKTAILS From Air Mail to Zombie, do you know your BACARDÍ alphabet? DISCOVER MORE OUR RECOMMENDED RUMS LIKE THIS? TRY THESE If you enjoy this rum, here’s our recommendations of other BACARDĺ rums we think you’ll like VIEW OUR RUMS BACARDÍ SPICED BACARDÍ RUM PUNCH BACARDÍ Carta Oro BACARDÍ COCONUT BACARDÍ AÑEJO CUATRO VIEW OUR RUMS MERCHANDISE GET KITTED OUT Glasses. Squeezers. Cocktail kits. Discover everything you need to create standout BACARDÍ cocktails. SHOP NOW FAQ CURIOUS ABOUT RUM? WHAT IS WHITE RUM? White rum is usually a light-bodied variant with a soft, sweet taste. It’s often used in light refreshing cocktails, especially favourites, like the Mojito and Daiquiri. IS RUM SWEET? Many think that, since rum is made from sugar cane, it must be sweet. But rum comes in a range of sweetness, from barely-there to an all-out sugar rush. HOW DO YOU MAKE RUM? Rum is made using only three ingredients: molasses from sugarcane, yeast and water. Ingredients are fermented, then distilled, aged, filtered and blended.
